response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, seasoned engineer with roughly 15 years of experience spanning web, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, including low-latency services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the role is a Principal-level (IC6) position specifically centered on experimentation, growth measurement, telemetry, and feature-flighting platforms, and the resume shows no experience with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, metrics infrastructure, or statistical analysis. His career trajectory is senior/staff IC on product and platform work at small-to-mid-size companies, without evidence of the multi-workstream, cross-org architectural leadership at enterprise scale that IC6 demands. Strong generalist, wrong specialization for this opening.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed backend systems
Proven work on high-scale, low-latency services (millions of HTTP requests/sec) and strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling)
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and developer-tooling experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows)
concerns: No demonstrated experience with experimentation platforms, feature flagging, growth engineering, or bias/rollout-risk analysis — the core of this role
No evidence of telemetry/metrics infrastructure ownership or statistical analysis work despite it being a required qualification
Scope gap to Principal/IC6: no track record of leading multi-team, cross-product initiatives at enterprise scale
